2604.17023.title: The Instrumental Dissolution of Typing: Why AI Challenges the Keyboard Era in Knowledge Work
2604.17023.paper_summary: For four decades, the QWERTY keyboard organized white-collar knowledge work. Typing's dominance was instrumental, not cognitively necessary. As multimodal AI achieves human-parity understanding of speech and gesture, this necessity dissolves. We introduce instrumental dissolution -- loss of institutional-default status while persisting in specialist niches. The keyboard era ends not through hardware replacement but through migration of its function into AI systems. The central contribution identifies the verification bottleneck: as AI collapses production friction, the primary constraint shifts from generation to evaluation. Knowledge workers become adversarial auditors rather than keystroke-producers. This restructures professional expertise, organizational communication, and how productive labor is recognized. Converging evidence from history, philosophy, neuroscience, technology, organizational studies, and cultural analysis supports this thesis. We map synthetic literacy -- oral input generating literate output -- as the defining feature of this transition. Under three scenarios (optimistic: 2028-2035; base: 2035-2045; pessimistic: 2045-2060), we specify disconfirmation criteria that would weaken the thesis if observed. We propose seven interface primitives operationalizing verification-centered HCI.
2604.17023.paper_summary_zh: 四十年來，QWERTY 鍵盤組織了白領知識工作。打字的主導地位是重要的，但並非認知上必要。隨著多模態 AI 實現對語音和手勢的人類平等理解，這種必要性逐漸消失。我們引入了工具性解體——在專業利基中持續存在的同時，失去了機構默認地位。鍵盤時代的結束不是通過硬體替換，而是其功能向 AI 系統的遷移。核心貢獻在於識別驗證瓶頸：隨著 AI 降低生產摩擦，主要限制從生成轉向評估。知識工作者變成了對抗性審核者，而不是擊鍵生產者。這重構了專業專長、組織溝通以及生產勞動的認可方式。來自歷史、哲學、神經科學、技術、組織研究和文化分析的匯聚證據支持這一論點。我們將合成素養——口頭輸入生成識字輸出——描繪為這一過渡的定義特徵。在三種情境下（樂觀：2028-2035；基準：2035-2045；悲觀：2045-2060），我們具體化了如果觀察到的將削弱論點的反駁標準。我們提出了七個界面原語，以操作化以驗證為中心的人機互動。
2604.17023.author: Wei Roy Hua et.al.
